$ /opt/conda/bin/python /home/jovyan/LLMOnto/Benchmark/OntologyConceptMatching/maseo_code/maseo/cqbycq/cli.py --config /home/jovyan/LLMOnto/Benchmark/OntologyConceptMatching/maseo_code/maseo/cqbycq/dataset/.run_config.yaml --cqs_file /home/jovyan/LLMOnto/Benchmark/OntologyConceptMatching/maseo_code/maseo/cqbycq/dataset/cqs/swo_cq2onto_cqs.json --save_file /home/jovyan/LLMOnto/Benchmark/OntologyConceptMatching/maseo_code/maseo/cqbycq/dataset/cqbycq/google_gemma-4-31b-it/ontology/swo_cq2onto_cqbycq_ontology.owl

======================================================================
[1/35] CQ04: Which of the named and published algorithms does this tool use?
======================================================================
<?xml version="1.0"?>
<rdf:RDF xml:base="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#"
         xmlns:owl="http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#"
         xmlns:rdf="http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#"
         xmlns:rdfs="http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#"
         xmlns:xsd="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#">

  <owl:Ontology rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#"/>

  <!-- owl:Class declarations -->

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Tool">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A software tool or system that implements specific computational methods.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Tool</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Algorithm">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A named and potentially published procedure or formula for solving a problem.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Algorithm</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

  <!-- owl:ObjectProperty declarations -->

<owl:ObjectProperty rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#usesAlgorithm">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">Relates a tool to the algorithm it employs.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">uses algorithm</rdfs:label>
  <rdfs:domain rdf:resource="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Tool"/>
  <rdfs:range rdf:resource="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Algorithm"/>
</owl:ObjectProperty>

  <!-- owl:DatatypeProperty declarations -->

<owl:DatatypeProperty rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#hasAlgorithmName">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">The official name of the algorithm.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">algorithm name</rdfs:label>
  <rdfs:domain rdf:resource="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Algorithm"/>
  <rdfs:range rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string"/>
  <rdf:type rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#FunctionalProperty"/>
</owl:DatatypeProperty>

<owl:DatatypeProperty rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#isPublished">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">Indicates whether the algorithm has been published in literature or a public repository.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">is published</rdfs:label>
  <rdfs:domain rdf:resource="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Algorithm"/>
  <rdfs:range rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#boolean"/>
  <rdf:type rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#FunctionalProperty"/>
</owl:DatatypeProperty>

</rdf:RDF>

[2/35] CQ05: Are there any modifications to the algorithm the tool uses?
======================================================================
<?xml version="1.0"?>
<rdf:RDF xml:base="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#"
         xmlns:owl="http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#"
         xmlns:rdf="http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#"
         xmlns:rdfs="http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#"
         xmlns:xsd="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#">

  <owl:Ontology rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#"/>

  <!-- owl:Class declarations -->

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Tool">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A software tool or system that implements specific computational methods.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Tool</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Algorithm">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A named and potentially published procedure or formula for solving a problem.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Algorithm</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#AlgorithmModification">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A reification class representing a specific modification of an algorithm as implemented within a tool.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Algorithm Modification</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

  <!-- owl:ObjectProperty declarations -->

<owl:ObjectProperty rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#usesAlgorithm">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">Relates a tool to the algorithm it employs.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">uses algorithm</rdfs:label>
  <rdfs:domain rdf:resource="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Tool"/>
  <rdfs:range rdf:resource="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Algorithm"/>
</owl:ObjectProperty>

<owl:ObjectProperty rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#hasModification">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">Relates a tool to the specific modifications it makes to an algorithm.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">has modification</rdfs:label>
  <rdfs:domain rdf:resource="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Tool"/>
  <rdfs:range rdf:resource="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#AlgorithmModification"/>
</owl:ObjectProperty>

<owl:ObjectProperty rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#modifiesAlgorithm">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">Relates a modification instance to the original algorithm being modified.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">modifies algorithm</rdfs:label>
  <rdfs:domain rdf:resource="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#AlgorithmModification"/>
  <rdfs:range rdf:resource="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Algorithm"/>
  <rdf:type rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#FunctionalProperty"/>
</owl:ObjectProperty>

  <!-- owl:DatatypeProperty declarations -->

<owl:DatatypeProperty rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#hasAlgorithmName">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">The official name of the algorithm.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">algorithm name</rdfs:label>
  <rdfs:domain rdf:resource="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Algorithm"/>
  <rdfs:range rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string"/>
  <rdf:type rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#FunctionalProperty"/>
</owl:DatatypeProperty>

<owl:DatatypeProperty rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#isPublished">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">Indicates whether the algorithm has been published in literature or a public repository.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">is published</rdfs:label>
  <rdfs:domain rdf:resource="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Algorithm"/>
  <rdfs:range rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#boolean"/>
  <rdf:type rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#FunctionalProperty"/>
</owl:DatatypeProperty>

<owl:DatatypeProperty rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#modificationDescription">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A textual description of the changes made to the algorithm.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">modification description</rdfs:label>
  <rdfs:domain rdf:resource="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#AlgorithmModification"/>
  <rdfs:range rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string"/>
</owl:DatatypeProperty>

[5/35] CQ08: What software can perform task x?
======================================================================
<?xml version="1.0"?>
<rdf:RDF xml:base="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#"
         xmlns:owl="http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#"
         xmlns:rdf="http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#"
         xmlns:rdfs="http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#"
         xmlns:xsd="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#">

  <owl:Ontology rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#"/>

  <!-- owl:Class declarations -->

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Tool">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A software tool or system that implements specific computational methods.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Tool</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Algorithm">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A named and potentially published procedure or formula for solving a problem.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Algorithm</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#AlgorithmModification">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A reification class representing a specific modification of an algorithm as implemented within a tool.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Algorithm Modification</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Feature">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A functional capability or characteristic provided by a software tool.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Feature</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#XmlEditing">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">The specific capability of editing XML documents.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">XML Editing</rdfs:label>
  <rdfs:subClassOf rdf:resource='http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Feature'/>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#SoftwareType">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">The category or delivery format of the software tool.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Software Type</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Api">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A software type that provides an Application Programming Interface.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">API</rdfs:label>
  <rdfs:subClassOf rdf:resource='http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#SoftwareType'/>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#DesktopApplication">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A software type that is installed and run locally on a desktop computer.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Desktop Application</rdfs:label>
  <rdfs:subClassOf rdf:resource='http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#SoftwareType'/>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Task">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A specific goal or operation that a user intends to accomplish using software.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Task</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

  <!-- owl:ObjectProperty declarations -->

<owl:ObjectProperty rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#usesAlgorithm">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">Relates a tool to the algorithm it employs.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">uses algorithm</rdfs:label>
  <rdfs:domain rdf:resource="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Tool"/>
  <rdfs:range rdf:resource="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Algorithm"/>
</owl:ObjectProperty>

  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">Relates a tool to the specific modifications it makes to an algorithm.</rdfs:comment>

  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Task</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Data">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">The output or dataset generated by a software tool.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Data</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

  <!-- owl:ObjectProperty declarations -->

</owl:Class>

[11/35] CQ19: What software can read a .cel file?

<?xml version="1.0"?>
<rdf:RDF xml:base="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#"
         xmlns:owl="http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#"
         xmlns:rdf="http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#"
         xmlns:rdfs="http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#"
         xmlns:xsd="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#">

  <owl:Ontology rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#"/>

  <!-- owl:Class declarations -->

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Tool">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A software tool or system that implements specific computational methods.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Tool</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Algorithm">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A named and potentially published procedure or formula for solving a problem.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Algorithm</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#AlgorithmModification">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A reification class representing a specific modification of an algorithm as implemented within a tool.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Algorithm Modification</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Feature">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A functional capability or characteristic provided by a software tool.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Feature</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#XmlEditing">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">The specific capability of editing XML documents.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">XML Editing</rdfs:label>
  <rdfs:subClassOf rdf:resource='http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Feature'/>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#SoftwareType">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">The category or delivery format of the software tool.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Software Type</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Api">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A software type that provides an Application Programming Interface.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">API</rdfs:label>
  <rdfs:subClassOf rdf:resource='http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#SoftwareType'/>
</owl:Class>

  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Desktop Application</rdfs:label>
  <rdfs:subClassOf rdf:resource='http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#SoftwareType'/>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#WebService">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A software type that is hosted on a server and accessible over a network.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Web Service</rdfs:label>
  <rdfs:subClassOf rdf:resource='http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#SoftwareType'/>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Task">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A specific goal or operation that a user intends to accomplish using software.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Task</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Data">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">The output or dataset generated by a software tool.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Data</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#DataToolTaskUsage">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A reification class representing the usage of specific data by a tool to support a specific task.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Data Tool Task Usage</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Format">

  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Format</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

======================================================================
[15/35] CQ26: What open source, maintained software can I use to process these in this format?
======================================================================
<?xml version="1.0"?>
<rdf:RDF xml:base="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#"
         xmlns:owl="http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#"
         xmlns:rdf="http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#"
         xmlns:rdfs="http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#"
         xmlns:xsd="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#">

  <owl:Ontology rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#"/>

  <!-- owl:Class declarations -->

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Tool">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A software tool or system that implements specific computational methods.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Tool</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Algorithm">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A named and potentially published procedure or formula for solving a problem.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Algorithm</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#AlgorithmModification">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A reification class representing a specific modification of an algorithm as implemented within a tool.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Algorithm Modification</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Feature">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A functional capability or characteristic provided by a software tool.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Feature</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#XmlEditing">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">The specific capability of editing XML documents.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">XML Editing</rdfs:label>
  <rdfs:subClassOf rdf:resource='http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Feature'/>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#SoftwareType">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">The category or delivery format of the software tool.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Software Type</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Api">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A software type that provides an Application Programming Interface.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">API</rdfs:label>
  <rdfs:subClassOf rdf:resource='http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#SoftwareType'/>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#DesktopApplication">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A software type that is installed and run locally on a desktop computer.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Desktop Application</rdfs:label>
  <rdfs:subClassOf rdf:resource='http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#SoftwareType'/>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#WebService">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A software type that is hosted on a server and accessible over a network.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Web Service</rdfs:label>
  <rdfs:subClassOf rdf:resource='http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#SoftwareType'/>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Task">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A specific goal or operation that a user intends to accomplish using software.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Task</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Data">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">The output or dataset generated by a software tool.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Data</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#DataToolTaskUsage">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A reification class representing the usage of specific data by a tool to support a specific task.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Data Tool Task Usage</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Format">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A specific data format or file extension supported by a tool (e.g., CSV, JSON, XML).</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Format</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#FormatType">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">The classification of a data format regarding its accessibility and ownership.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Format Type</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#ProprietaryFormat">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A format owned by a specific entity and not openly documented.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Proprietary Format</rdfs:label>
  <rdfs:subClassOf rdf:resource='http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#FormatType'/>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#OpenFormat">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A format that is publicly documented and available for use without restriction.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Open Format</rdfs:label>
  <rdfs:subClassOf rdf:resource='http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#FormatType'/>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#ToolDataCompatibility">

  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Tool Data Compatibility</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">modification description</rdfs:label>
  <rdfs:domain rdf:resource="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#AlgorithmModification"/>
  <rdfs:range rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string"/>
</owl:DatatypeProperty>

<owl:DatatypeProperty rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#hasFormatExtension">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">The file extension associated with the format (e.g., '.cel', '.csv').</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">format extension</rdfs:label>
  <rdfs:domain rdf:resource="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Format"/>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#WebService">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A software type that is hosted on a server and accessible over a network.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Web Service</rdfs:label>
  <rdfs:subClassOf rdf:resource='http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#SoftwareType'/>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Task">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A specific goal or operation that a user intends to accomplish using software.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Task</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Data">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">The output or dataset generated by a software tool.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Data</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#DataToolTaskUsage">

  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Data Tool Task Usage</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Format">

  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Format</rdfs:label>

  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Format Type</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Proprietary Format</rdfs:label>

</owl:Class>

[18/35] CQ32: How do I get help?
======================================================================

         xmlns:owl="http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#"

  <owl:Ontology rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#"/>

  <!-- owl:Class declarations -->

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Tool">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A software tool or system that implements specific computational methods.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Tool</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Algorithm">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A named and potentially published procedure or formula for solving a problem.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Algorithm</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#AlgorithmModification">

  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Algorithm Modification</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

[19/35] CQ36: Who developed it?

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Tool">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A software tool or system that implements specific computational methods.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Tool</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Algorithm">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A named and potentially published procedure or formula for solving a problem.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Algorithm</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

======================================================================
[20/35] CQ37: What is the homepage of the software?
======================================================================

         xmlns:owl="http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#"

  <!-- owl:Class declarations -->

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Tool">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A software tool or system that implements specific computational methods.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Tool</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Algorithm">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A named and potentially published procedure or formula for solving a problem.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Algorithm</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Algorithm Modification</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

======================================================================
[21/35] CQ38: Can we collaborate with developers of software x?
======================================================================

[22/35] CQ40: Which URL can I get it from?
======================================================================

         xmlns:owl="http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#"
         xmlns:rdf="http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#"

         xmlns:xsd="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#">

  <owl:Ontology rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#"/>

  <!-- owl:Class declarations -->

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Tool">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A software tool or system that implements specific computational methods.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Tool</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Algorithm">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A named and potentially published procedure or formula for solving a problem.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Algorithm</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#AlgorithmModification">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A reification class representing a specific modification of an algorithm as implemented within a tool.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Algorithm Modification</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Feature">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A functional capability or characteristic provided by a software tool.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Feature</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

[23/35] CQ45: How long has this software been around?
======================================================================

  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Tool</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

  <rdfs:range rdf:resource="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Feature"/>
</owl:ObjectProperty>

<owl:ObjectProperty rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#hasSoftwareType">

[7/35] CQ14: Does it do what I want or need it to do e.g. render a gif?
======================================================================
<?xml version="1.0"?>
<rdf:RDF xml:base="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#"
         xmlns:owl="http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#"
         xmlns:rdf="http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#"
         xmlns:rdfs="http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#"
         xmlns:xsd="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#">

  <owl:Ontology rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#"/>

  <!-- owl:Class declarations -->

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Tool">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A software tool or system that implements specific computational methods.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Tool</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Algorithm">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A named and published computational procedure or formula for solving a problem.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Algorithm</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Publication">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A document or venue where an algorithm is formally published.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Publication</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#AlgorithmUsage">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A reification class representing the specific application of an algorithm within a tool, allowing for the description of modifications.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Algorithm Usage</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Feature">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A specific capability or functionality provided by a software tool.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Feature</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#SoftwareType">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">The category or architectural type of software (e.g., API, Desktop application, Web application).</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Software Type</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#WebService">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A software type that provides services over a network, typically via HTTP.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Web Service</rdfs:label>
  <rdfs:subClassOf rdf:resource='http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#SoftwareType'/>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Task">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A specific goal or activity that a software tool is designed to perform.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Task</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

  <!-- owl:ObjectProperty declarations -->

<owl:ObjectProperty rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#usesAlgorithm">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">Relates a tool to the algorithm it employs.</rdfs:comment>

  <rdfs:range rdf:resource="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#AlgorithmUsage"/>

  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">employs algorithm</rdfs:label>
  <rdfs:domain rdf:resource="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#AlgorithmUsage"/>

</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#ToolDataTaskUsage">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A reification class that links a software tool, the dataset it can process, and the task it supports.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Tool Data Task Usage</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

  <!-- owl:ObjectProperty declarations -->

  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">modification description</rdfs:label>

[10/35] CQ17: What are the input and output formats for this software?
======================================================================
<?xml version="1.0"?>
<rdf:RDF xml:base="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#"
         xmlns:owl="http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#"
         xmlns:rdf="http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#"
         xmlns:rdfs="http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#"
         xmlns:xsd="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#">

  <owl:Ontology rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#"/>

  <!-- owl:Class declarations -->

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Tool">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A software tool or system that implements specific computational methods.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Tool</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Algorithm">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A named and published computational procedure or formula for solving a problem.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Algorithm</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Publication">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A document or venue where an algorithm is formally published.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Publication</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#AlgorithmUsage">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A reification class representing the specific application of an algorithm within a tool, allowing for the description of modifications.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Algorithm Usage</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Feature">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A specific capability or functionality provided by a software tool.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Feature</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#SoftwareType">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">The category or architectural type of software (e.g., API, Desktop application, Web application).</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Software Type</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#WebService">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A software type that provides services over a network, typically via HTTP.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Web Service</rdfs:label>
  <rdfs:subClassOf rdf:resource='http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#SoftwareType'/>
</owl:Class>

</owl:Class>

  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Tool Data Task Usage</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Format">

  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Format</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

  <owl:Ontology rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#"/>

  <!-- owl:Class declarations -->

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Tool">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A software tool or system that implements specific computational methods.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Tool</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Algorithm">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A named and published computational procedure or formula for solving a problem.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Algorithm</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Publication">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A document or venue where an algorithm is formally published.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Publication</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#AlgorithmUsage">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A reification class representing the specific application of an algorithm within a tool, allowing for the description of modifications.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Algorithm Usage</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Feature">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A specific capability or functionality provided by a software tool.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Feature</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#SoftwareType">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">The category or architectural type of software (e.g., API, Desktop application, Web application).</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Software Type</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#WebService">

</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#ToolDataTaskUsage">

  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Tool Data Task Usage</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Format">

  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Format</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#ValidInput">

  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Valid Input</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

[13/35] CQ22: Can this software export from its proprietary data format to an open format such as csv, txt?
======================================================================
<?xml version="1.0"?>
<rdf:RDF xml:base="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#"
         xmlns:owl="http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#"
         xmlns:rdf="http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#"
         xmlns:rdfs="http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#"
         xmlns:xsd="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#">

  <owl:Ontology rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#"/>

  <!-- owl:Class declarations -->

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Tool">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A software tool or system that implements specific computational methods.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Tool</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Algorithm">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A named and published computational procedure or formula for solving a problem.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Algorithm</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Publication">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A document or venue where an algorithm is formally published.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Publication</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#AlgorithmUsage">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A reification class representing the specific application of an algorithm within a tool, allowing for the description of modifications.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Algorithm Usage</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Feature">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A specific capability or functionality provided by a software tool.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Feature</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#SoftwareType">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">The category or architectural type of software (e.g., API, Desktop application, Web application).</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Software Type</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#WebService">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A software type that provides services over a network, typically via HTTP.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Web Service</rdfs:label>
  <rdfs:subClassOf rdf:resource='http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#SoftwareType'/>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Task">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A specific goal or activity that a software tool is designed to perform.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Task</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Dataset">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A collection of data produced by a software tool.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Dataset</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#ToolDataTaskUsage">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A reification class that links a software tool, the dataset it can process, and the task it supports.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Tool Data Task Usage</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Format">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A specific data format or file specification (e.g., RDF, XML, JSON, CSV).</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Format</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#ValidInput">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A reification class that defines the requirements for a valid input to a tool, combining format and dataset constraints.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Valid Input</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Format Category</rdfs:label>

======================================================================
[14/35] CQ23: Can software A work with data that are output from software B?
======================================================================
<?xml version="1.0"?>
<rdf:RDF xml:base="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#"
         xmlns:owl="http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#"
         xmlns:rdf="http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#"
         xmlns:rdfs="http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#"
         xmlns:xsd="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#">

  <owl:Ontology rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#"/>

  <!-- owl:Class declarations -->

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Tool">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A software tool or system that implements specific computational methods.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Tool</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Algorithm">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A named and published computational procedure or formula for solving a problem.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Algorithm</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Publication">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A document or venue where an algorithm is formally published.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Publication</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#AlgorithmUsage">

  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Algorithm Usage</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Software Type</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">Relates a tool to its valid input specifications.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">has valid input</rdfs:label>

  <rdfs:range rdf:resource="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#ValidInput"/>
</owl:ObjectProperty>

<owl:ObjectProperty rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#requiresFormat">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">Relates a valid input specification to the required data format.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">requires format</rdfs:label>
  <rdfs:domain rdf:resource="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#ValidInput"/>
  <rdfs:range rdf:resource="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Format"/>
</owl:ObjectProperty>

<owl:ObjectProperty rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#requiresDataset">

  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">requires dataset</rdfs:label>
  <rdfs:domain rdf:resource="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#ValidInput"/>
  <rdfs:range rdf:resource="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Dataset"/>
</owl:ObjectProperty>

  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">has format category</rdfs:label>

  <rdfs:range rdf:resource="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#FormatCategory"/>
</owl:ObjectProperty>

<owl:ObjectProperty rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#canProcessOutputOf">

  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">can process output of</rdfs:label>

  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">file extension</rdfs:label>

======================================================================
[15/35] CQ26: What open source, maintained software can I use to process these in this format?
======================================================================
<?xml version="1.0"?>
<rdf:RDF xml:base="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#"
         xmlns:owl="http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#"
         xmlns:rdf="http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#"
         xmlns:rdfs="http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#"
         xmlns:xsd="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#">

  <owl:Ontology rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#"/>

  <!-- owl:Class declarations -->

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Tool">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A software tool or system that implements specific computational methods.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Tool</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Algorithm">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A named and published computational procedure or formula for solving a problem.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Algorithm</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Publication">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A document or venue where an algorithm is formally published.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Publication</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#AlgorithmUsage">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A reification class representing the specific application of an algorithm within a tool, allowing for the description of modifications.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Algorithm Usage</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Feature">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A specific capability or functionality provided by a software tool.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Feature</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#SoftwareType">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">The category or architectural type of software (e.g., API, Desktop application, Web application).</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Software Type</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#WebService">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A software type that provides services over a network, typically via HTTP.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Web Service</rdfs:label>
  <rdfs:subClassOf rdf:resource='http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#SoftwareType'/>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Task">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A specific goal or activity that a software tool is designed to perform.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Task</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Dataset">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A collection of data produced by a software tool.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Dataset</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#ToolDataTaskUsage">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A reification class that links a software tool, the dataset it can process, and the task it supports.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Tool Data Task Usage</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Format">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A specific data format or file specification (e.g., RDF, XML, JSON, CSV).</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Format</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#ValidInput">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A reification class that defines the requirements for a valid input to a tool, combining format and dataset constraints.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Valid Input</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#FormatCategory">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">The classification of a data format, such as Open or Proprietary.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Format Category</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#ToolCompatibility">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A reification class representing the compatibility between two tools, where one tool can process the output of another.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Tool Compatibility</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#LicenseType">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">The type of license under which a software tool is released (e.g., Open Source, Proprietary).</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">License Type</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

  <!-- owl:ObjectProperty declarations -->

  <rdf:type rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#FunctionalProperty"/>
</owl:DatatypeProperty>

  <!-- owl:Class declarations -->

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Tool">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A software tool or system that implements specific computational methods.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Tool</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Algorithm">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A named and published computational procedure or formula for solving a problem.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Algorithm</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Publication</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#AlgorithmUsage">

  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Algorithm Usage</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

======================================================================
[17/35] CQ30: Where can I get the software?
======================================================================
<?xml version="1.0"?>
<rdf:RDF xml:base="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#"
         xmlns:owl="http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#"
         xmlns:rdf="http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#"
         xmlns:rdfs="http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#"
         xmlns:xsd="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#">

  <owl:Ontology rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#"/>

  <!-- owl:Class declarations -->

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Tool">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A software tool or system that implements specific computational methods.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Tool</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Algorithm">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A named and published computational procedure or formula for solving a problem.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Algorithm</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Publication">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A document or venue where an algorithm is formally published.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Publication</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#AlgorithmUsage">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A reification class representing the specific application of an algorithm within a tool, allowing for the description of modifications.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Algorithm Usage</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Feature">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A specific capability or functionality provided by a software tool.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Feature</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#SoftwareType">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">The category or architectural type of software (e.g., API, Desktop application, Web application).</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Software Type</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#WebService">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A software type that provides services over a network, typically via HTTP.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Web Service</rdfs:label>
  <rdfs:subClassOf rdf:resource='http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#SoftwareType'/>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Task">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A specific goal or activity that a software tool is designed to perform.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Task</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Dataset">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A collection of data produced by a software tool.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Dataset</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#ToolDataTaskUsage">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A reification class that links a software tool, the dataset it can process, and the task it supports.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Tool Data Task Usage</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Format">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A specific data format or file specification (e.g., RDF, XML, JSON, CSV).</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Format</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#ValidInput">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A reification class that defines the requirements for a valid input to a tool, combining format and dataset constraints.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Valid Input</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#FormatCategory">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">The classification of a data format, such as Open or Proprietary.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Format Category</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#ToolCompatibility">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A reification class representing the compatibility between two tools, where one tool can process the output of another.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Tool Compatibility</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#LicenseType">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">The type of license under which a software tool is released (e.g., Open Source, Proprietary).</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">License Type</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#ToolVersion">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A specific released version of a software tool.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Tool Version</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#ToolVersionExport">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A reification class that links a specific tool version, the input it processes, and the resulting data export.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Tool Version Export</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

  <!-- owl:ObjectProperty declarations -->

[18/35] CQ32: How do I get help?
======================================================================
<?xml version="1.0"?>
<rdf:RDF xml:base="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#"
         xmlns:owl="http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#"
         xmlns:rdf="http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#"
         xmlns:rdfs="http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#"
         xmlns:xsd="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#">

  <owl:Ontology rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#"/>

  <!-- owl:Class declarations -->

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Tool">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A software tool or system that implements specific computational methods.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Tool</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Algorithm">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A named and published computational procedure or formula for solving a problem.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Algorithm</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Publication">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A document or venue where an algorithm is formally published.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Publication</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#AlgorithmUsage">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A reification class representing the specific application of an algorithm within a tool, allowing for the description of modifications.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Algorithm Usage</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Feature">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A specific capability or functionality provided by a software tool.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Feature</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#SoftwareType">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">The category or architectural type of software (e.g., API, Desktop application, Web application).</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Software Type</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#WebService">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A software type that provides services over a network, typically via HTTP.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Web Service</rdfs:label>
  <rdfs:subClassOf rdf:resource='http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#SoftwareType'/>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Task">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A specific goal or activity that a software tool is designed to perform.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Task</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Dataset">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A collection of data produced by a software tool.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Dataset</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#ToolDataTaskUsage">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A reification class that links a software tool, the dataset it can process, and the task it supports.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Tool Data Task Usage</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Format">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A specific data format or file specification (e.g., RDF, XML, JSON, CSV).</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Format</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#ValidInput">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A reification class that defines the requirements for a valid input to a tool, combining format and dataset constraints.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Valid Input</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#FormatCategory">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">The classification of a data format, such as Open or Proprietary.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Format Category</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#ToolCompatibility">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A reification class representing the compatibility between two tools, where one tool can process the output of another.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Tool Compatibility</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#LicenseType">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">The type of license under which a software tool is released (e.g., Open Source, Proprietary).</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">License Type</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#ToolVersion">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A specific released version of a software tool.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Tool Version</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#ToolVersionExport">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A reification class that links a specific tool version, the input it processes, and the resulting data export.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Tool Version Export</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#HelpResource">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A resource that provides assistance, documentation, or support for a software tool.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Help Resource</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

[19/35] CQ36: Who developed it?
======================================================================
<?xml version="1.0"?>
<rdf:RDF xml:base="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#"
         xmlns:owl="http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#"
         xmlns:rdf="http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#"
         xmlns:rdfs="http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#"
         xmlns:xsd="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#">

  <owl:Ontology rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#"/>

  <!-- owl:Class declarations -->

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Tool">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A software tool or system that implements specific computational methods.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Tool</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Algorithm">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A named and published computational procedure or formula for solving a problem.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Algorithm</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Publication">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A document or venue where an algorithm is formally published.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Publication</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#AlgorithmUsage">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A reification class representing the specific application of an algorithm within a tool, allowing for the description of modifications.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Algorithm Usage</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Feature">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A specific capability or functionality provided by a software tool.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Feature</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#SoftwareType">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">The category or architectural type of software (e.g., API, Desktop application, Web application).</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Software Type</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#WebService">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A software type that provides services over a network, typically via HTTP.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Web Service</rdfs:label>
  <rdfs:subClassOf rdf:resource='http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#SoftwareType'/>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Task">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A specific goal or activity that a software tool is designed to perform.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Task</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Dataset">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A collection of data produced by a software tool.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Dataset</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#ToolDataTaskUsage">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A reification class that links a software tool, the dataset it can process, and the task it supports.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Tool Data Task Usage</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Format">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A specific data format or file specification (e.g., RDF, XML, JSON, CSV).</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Format</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#ValidInput">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A reification class that defines the requirements for a valid input to a tool, combining format and dataset constraints.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Valid Input</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#FormatCategory">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">The classification of a data format, such as Open or Proprietary.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Format Category</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#ToolCompatibility">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A reification class representing the compatibility between two tools, where one tool can process the output of another.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Tool Compatibility</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#LicenseType">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">The type of license under which a software tool is released (e.g., Open Source, Proprietary).</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">License Type</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#ToolVersion">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A specific released version of a software tool.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Tool Version</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#ToolVersionExport">

  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Tool Version Export</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Developer</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

[20/35] CQ37: What is the homepage of the software?
======================================================================
<?xml version="1.0"?>
<rdf:RDF xml:base="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#"
         xmlns:owl="http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#"
         xmlns:rdf="http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#"
         xmlns:rdfs="http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#"
         xmlns:xsd="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#">

  <owl:Ontology rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#"/>

  <!-- owl:Class declarations -->

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Tool">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A software tool or system that implements specific computational methods.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Tool</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Algorithm">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A named and published computational procedure or formula for solving a problem.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Algorithm</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Publication">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A document or venue where an algorithm is formally published.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Publication</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#AlgorithmUsage">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A reification class representing the specific application of an algorithm within a tool, allowing for the description of modifications.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Algorithm Usage</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Feature">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A specific capability or functionality provided by a software tool.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Feature</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#SoftwareType">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">The category or architectural type of software (e.g., API, Desktop application, Web application).</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Software Type</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#WebService">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A software type that provides services over a network, typically via HTTP.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Web Service</rdfs:label>
  <rdfs:subClassOf rdf:resource='http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#SoftwareType'/>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Task">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A specific goal or activity that a software tool is designed to perform.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Task</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Dataset">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A collection of data produced by a software tool.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Dataset</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#ToolDataTaskUsage">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A reification class that links a software tool, the dataset it can process, and the task it supports.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Tool Data Task Usage</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Format">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A specific data format or file specification (e.g., RDF, XML, JSON, CSV).</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Format</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#ValidInput">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A reification class that defines the requirements for a valid input to a tool, combining format and dataset constraints.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Valid Input</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#FormatCategory">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">The classification of a data format, such as Open or Proprietary.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Format Category</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#ToolCompatibility">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A reification class representing the compatibility between two tools, where one tool can process the output of another.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Tool Compatibility</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#LicenseType">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">The type of license under which a software tool is released (e.g., Open Source, Proprietary).</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">License Type</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#ToolVersion">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A specific released version of a software tool.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Tool Version</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

======================================================================
[21/35] CQ38: Can we collaborate with developers of software x?
======================================================================
<?xml version="1.0"?>
<rdf:RDF xml:base="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#"
         xmlns:owl="http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#"
         xmlns:rdf="http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#"
         xmlns:rdfs="http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#"
         xmlns:xsd="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#">

  <owl:Ontology rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#"/>

  <!-- owl:Class declarations -->

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Tool">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A software tool or system that implements specific computational methods.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Tool</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Algorithm">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A named and published computational procedure or formula for solving a problem.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Algorithm</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Publication">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A document or venue where an algorithm is formally published.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Publication</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#AlgorithmUsage">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A reification class representing the specific application of an algorithm within a tool, allowing for the description of modifications.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Algorithm Usage</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Feature">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A specific capability or functionality provided by a software tool.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Feature</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#SoftwareType">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">The category or architectural type of software (e.g., API, Desktop application, Web application).</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Software Type</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#WebService">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A software type that provides services over a network, typically via HTTP.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Web Service</rdfs:label>
  <rdfs:subClassOf rdf:resource='http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#SoftwareType'/>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Task">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A specific goal or activity that a software tool is designed to perform.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Task</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Dataset">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A collection of data produced by a software tool.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Dataset</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#ToolDataTaskUsage">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A reification class that links a software tool, the dataset it can process, and the task it supports.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Tool Data Task Usage</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Format">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A specific data format or file specification (e.g., RDF, XML, JSON, CSV).</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Format</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#ValidInput">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A reification class that defines the requirements for a valid input to a tool, combining format and dataset constraints.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Valid Input</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#FormatCategory">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">The classification of a data format, such as Open or Proprietary.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Format Category</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#ToolCompatibility">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A reification class representing the compatibility between two tools, where one tool can process the output of another.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Tool Compatibility</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#LicenseType">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">The type of license under which a software tool is released (e.g., Open Source, Proprietary).</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">License Type</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#ToolVersion">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A specific released version of a software tool.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Tool Version</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#ToolVersionExport">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A reification class that links a specific tool version, the input it processes, and the resulting data export.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Tool Version Export</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#HelpResource">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A resource that provides assistance, documentation, or support for a software tool.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Help Resource</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Developer">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">An individual or organization responsible for the creation and development of a software tool.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Developer</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Collaborator">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">An individual or organization that seeks or engages in collaboration with developers.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Collaborator</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>

<owl:Class rdf:about="http://www.semanticweb.org/myontology#Collaboration">
  <rdfs:comment xml:lang="en">A reification class representing a collaboration relationship between a collaborator and a developer.</rdfs:comment>
  <rdfs:label xml:lang="en">Collaboration</rdfs:label>
</owl:Class>
